vertices.label: TACTILITY
vertices.title: Tactility (Physical Backlash)
vertices.description: A response to digital fatigue and AI generation. Artists are combining physical woodblock prints, ceramics, weaving, and tactile materials with algorithmic CAD templates to build authentic 'slow-made' works that highlight human labor.

vertices.label: AI-AGENTIC
vertices.title: AI-Agentic Integration
vertices.description: AI is transitioning from simple text prompt visualization to an active partner in design. Artists explore agentic models, simulate public discourse ecosystems, hack neural weights, and build custom generative frameworks.

vertices.label: ECOPOLITICS
vertices.title: Ecopolitics (Biophilic Focus)
vertices.description: Embedding environmental data and biological systems into interactive installations. Living moss, mycelium sensors, and climate models reflect real-time planetary dynamics, transforming installations into restorative civic sanctuaries.

vertices.label: RELATIONAL
vertices.title: Relationality & Low-tech Nets
vertices.description: Curations like Transmediale's 'Mango Belt' challenge centralized, high-speed corporate infrastructures. They explore low-bandwidth community networks, digital sovereignty, and relational protocols adapted to local needs.

vertices.label: HYBRIDITY
vertices.title: Hybridity (Spatial Mixed Reality)
vertices.description: Blending live theater with spatial computing. Forums like Ars Electronica's 'Staged Realities' combine real-time motion capture, AI actors, and physical performers, altering the boundaries of performance authorship.
